message:
test(fuzz): add ObjectId::new keyed-hash domain-separation fuzz target (resolves nnp3h)

ObjectId::new (object.rs:61-68) is the keyed-hash content-addressing
primitive. Existing fuzz coverage tests consumers (object_id_verifier,
iblt_decode, mesh_post_verify_*) but not the constructor itself; a
regression that drops one of the four mix steps (FCP2-OBJECT-V2 domain
tag, zone, schema hash, keyed-hash) would silently break a binding.

8 properties asserted:
- determinism, content-binding, zone-binding, schema-binding,
  key-binding, parse_prefixed round-trip
- domain separation from from_unscoped_bytes (keyed vs unkeyed)
- panic-free construction over arbitrary inputs

Once-gated regression anchors:
- empty-content scoped vs unscoped MUST differ
- separator-aliasing SchemaId pair (a:b/c vs a/b:c) MUST hash distinct
  (anchors the mzi9x length-prefixing fix at SchemaId::hash)
- keyed-hash actually keys (guards against new_keyed → new regression)

Picked up rustfmt import-ordering drift in three previously-shipped
fuzz targets along the way.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -447,5 +447,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_object_id_construction"
+path = "fuzz_targets/object_id_construction.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
